timeline_html:
Summary. Lisa began noticing shedding around February 2026, with hair loss coming in waves since then. Several life and health factors have overlapped, making the pattern feel unpredictable.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Major illness with fever (ongoing; date not provided)
  • Started or stopped hormonal treatment (ongoing; date not provided)
  • Major emotional stress (ongoing; date not provided)
  • Chronic stress (ongoing; date not provided)
  • Surgery or hospitalization (resolved; date not provided)
  • Chronic illness flare (ongoing; date not provided)
  • Over-exercising (resolved; date not provided)
  • Perimenopause symptoms (ongoing; date not provided)

Timeline forecast. Based on her reported timing, shedding likely reached its worst around March 2026 and should gradually calm through May–July 2026, with baby hairs appearing from early summer onward. Because chronic stress and hormonal factors are still active, improvement may unfold more slowly, but stabilization is expected as these ease. Dominant driver: chronic stress and hormonal imbalance, as these are ongoing and align with the current shedding waves.

Where you are now. You’re moving through the stabilization phase — shedding may still fluctuate, but early regrowth has already started showing. Keep supporting your body’s recovery; with steadier routines and stress relief, stronger strands will follow. 🌱
